issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

# SECURITY REVIEW: chrome-devtools-mcp import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** DEV-306 (e9064ddd-0db4-485b-ad28-72870a86e292)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/ChromeDevTools/chrome-devtools-mcp
**Org:** ChromeDevTools (Google official)
**License:** Apache-2.0
**Stars:** 44,168 | Last push: 2026-06-21
**Type:** MCP server (stdio transport, Puppeteer/Node.js)

## What to review

### Protocol surface exposed
- MCP stdio server exposing Chrome DevTools Protocol via Puppeteer
- Tools likely include: `navigate`, `click`, `screenshot`, `inspect_dom`, `read_console`, `execute_js`, `monitor_network`
- Read-mostly; `execute_js` is a write capability (executes arbitrary JS in browser page context)

### Duplication check
- No existing browser-automation or Chrome DevTools MCP in workspace skill list — no duplicate

### Install footprint
- `npm i @chrome-devtools/mcp-server`
- Puppeteer downloads Chromium (~300MB) at install time
- Runtime: Node.js only, no Python, no OS-level permissions beyond what Chromium needs (sandbox mode)
- No OAuth, no external API calls, no credential storage

### Maintenance signal
- 44,168 GitHub stars
- ChromeDevTools org = official Google/Chrome DevTools team
- Active: last push 2026-06-21 (yesterday)
- Likely low open issue count for a project of this maturity

### Security concerns to verify
1. **execute_js scope**: Verify that JS execution is confined to the Puppeteer-controlled browser instance; should NOT have access to agent filesystem or host network beyond what the controlled browser session targets
2. **Chromium sandbox**: Verify Puppeteer runs with `--no-sandbox` only if explicitly required (flag as risk if so)
3. **Network access**: MCP server connects only to local Puppeteer/Chrome instance (localhost); no third-party data exfil
4. **Supply chain**: Apache-2.0, official Google org — low risk

### Required scopes
- Disk: read-only for Chromium binary, optional screenshot write to local path
- Network: localhost only (Chrome DevTools Protocol on 127.0.0.1)
- No OAuth, no API keys

## Decision required

Set `decision=green` on DEV-306 when review passes, then promote DEV-306 to `todo`.
If red: set `decision=red` and pin `blocked_reason=security_review_failed` to DEV-306.
issues.title: SECURITY REVIEW: chrome-devtools-mcp import

## Input

Signal from 2026-06-21 Workspace Upgrade Report (DEV-290):
- 10 issues carry `retry_log` metadata indicating stall-and-retry cycles:
  - DEV-264 (workspace-operations-manager): `{"attempts":2,"last_retry":"2026-06-15T07:49:02Z","reason":"stall >90min no comments"}`
  - DEV-257 (quality-gate-manager): QA Gate: DEV-187 stalled
  - DEV-223 (benchmark-agent/research-scout): BENCHMARK ACTION stalled
  - DEV-77, DEV-62, DEV-61, DEV-63 (research-scout): vendor/MCP research tasks stalled
  - DEV-47, DEV-50 (skill-builder): skill creation tasks stalled
  - DEV-40 (documentation-agent): User Guide task stalled
- Pattern: research-heavy tasks and QA gate verifications stall >90min with no comments emitted before retry.
- Root cause hypothesis: these agent prompts lack explicit instructions to (a) emit a progress comment within 30 min of starting, and (b) post a blocked comment if unable to proceed, rather than silently hanging.

## Expected Output

Prompt patches for the 4 most affected agents (workspace-operations-manager, quality-gate-manager, research-scout, skill-builder) that:
1. Add an explicit "emit a progress comment within 30 minutes of starting" rule.
2. Add an explicit "post a blocked comment with reason if unable to proceed — never run silently past 60 min" rule.
3. Do NOT break any existing behavior or add unnecessary verbosity to short-running tasks.

Concrete deliverables:
- Read each agent's current instructions via `multica agent get <id> --output json`.
- Draft the minimal prompt patch (3–5 lines added to each prompt).
- Apply the patch via `multica agent update <id> --instructions-file <path>` (or equivalent).
- Post a diff summary as a comment on DEV-290.

## Acceptance Criteria
- workspace-operations-manager, quality-gate-manager, research-scout, and skill-builder prompts updated with progress-comment and blocked-comment rules.
- No change to any other agent prompt.
- Diff summary posted on DEV-290.
- The patch is minimal — maximum 5 new lines per agent prompt.

## Dependencies
- `multica agent update` CLI must support `--instructions` or `--instructions-file` flag.
- Read existing agent prompts before writing any patch.

## Risk
- Over-verbose instructions could increase comment noise. Keep the patch tight and conditional ("if >30 min have elapsed since last comment").
- Wrong instruction wording could cause agents to spam comments on fast-running tasks. Test wording against DEV-264 scenario.

## Definition of Done
- 4 agent prompts patched.
- Comment diff posted on DEV-290.
- No new retry_log entries appear for the patched agents in the next week's upgrade report.
issues.title: Workspace Upgrade Action — Fix retry-loop prompt for research/QA stall agents

## SECURITY REVIEW: supabase-mcp-setup import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** [DEV-242](mention://issue/46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/supabase/mcp (v0.8.2)
**License:** Apache-2.0

### Install footprint
- HTTP MCP server at `https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp` — no local process
- Self-hosted option: `http://localhost:54321/mcp` (Supabase CLI)
- Requires: Supabase personal access token OR OAuth 2.1 flow
- Client-side config: `.mcp.json` entry pointing to the MCP URL

### Required scopes
- **PAT path:** Personal access token with `supabase:mcp` scope (full project access by default)
- **OAuth path:** `supabase:mcp` OAuth scope
- **Write capabilities (default, not read_only):** execute arbitrary SQL, create/drop tables, create users, manage auth, deploy edge functions
- **Read-only mode (recommended):** `?read_only=true` restricts to SELECT and read operations
- **Project scoping:** `?project_ref=<ref>` limits to one project

### Security checklist for auditor
1. **Token handling:** Confirm the skill template stores PATs only as env vars (never in `.mcp.json` or committed files)
2. **Default posture:** Verify skill enforces `read_only=true` as the documented default and requires explicit user approval to remove
3. **Scope documentation:** Confirm that write-mode removal requires workspace-owner approval per Security Rules (all write paths to external systems)
4. **Token revocation path:** Verify the skill documents how to revoke a PAT if compromised
5. **Self-hosted vs cloud:** Confirm self-hosted local MCP is lower risk than cloud MCP (no external data transmission)
6. **License compliance:** Apache-2.0 permits use in our Skill Library

### Expected outcome
- `decision=green` if skill template defaults to read-only, no PAT in config files, revocation path documented
- `decision=yellow` if write-mode default but mitigations documented (downgrade to explicit warning in skill)
- `decision=red` if PAT leakage risk or no scoping mechanism

**On green:** Set `multica issue metadata set 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 --key decision --value green`, then promote: `multica issue status 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 todo`
**On red:** Set `multica issue metadata set 46cddd52-275d-446c-ba7f-54a890830fc2 --key blocked_reason --value security_review_failed`
issues.title: SECURITY REVIEW: supabase-mcp-setup import

Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-06-23 07:00 Asia/Kuwait. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Daily Command Center Digest — 2026-06-23

    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.

## Expected Output

A prompt patch to quality-gate-manager instructions that:
1. Strengthens the fast-path trigger: adds explicit examples of matching title patterns including "Daily Factory Health Audit —" (hyphen variant observed in DEV-251).
2. Adds a priority note: "Process autopilot fast-path FIRST before any standard quality gate work to prevent queue accumulation."
3. Adds a sweep clause: "On each QGM run, sweep all in_review issues — not just the one that triggered this run — and apply the fast-path to all autopilot-pattern issues found in a single pass."
4. Result: a concrete `multica agent update <qgm-id> --instructions "<patched text>"` command, or a description of the exact text delta to insert.

- [ ] Patched instructions text written and reviewed for correctness
- [ ] Title pattern list is exhaustive: "Daily Command Center Digest —", "Factory Health Audit —", "Daily Factory Health Audit —", "Workspace Upgrade Report —"

- No external blockers

Medium. Modifying QGM instructions affects all in_review quality gates. Risk: too-broad fast-path accidentally closes real shippable work. Mitigation: fast-path only applies when BOTH criteria A (autopilot assignee) AND B (title/description pattern) match simultaneously.

- QGM instructions updated
- Fast-path sweep clause present
- Stale in_review count drops by ≥10 on the next QGM run
- Result comment on this issue

## SECURITY REVIEW: sandbox-process-runner import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** [DEV-241](mention://issue/14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime
**License:** Apache-2.0

### Install footprint
- npm package: `@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` (global install)
- Installs `srt` CLI binary
- Wraps target process with macOS `sandbox-exec` or Linux `bubblewrap`
- Intercepts network via proxy-based filtering (man-in-the-middle for TLS inspection)
- No persistent daemon; process lifecycle tied to the sandboxed command

### Required scopes
- Root/sudo NOT required on Linux (bubblewrap is user-namespaces based)
- macOS: uses `sandbox-exec` (built-in macOS API, no elevated privileges)
- Network proxy: runs on loopback, intercepts outbound HTTP/HTTPS via LD_PRELOAD or system proxy env vars

### Security checklist for auditor
1. **Supply chain:** Confirm `@anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime` is the official Anthropic package (not a typosquat)
2. **TLS proxy risk:** The package performs MITM on TLS to enforce network restrictions — verify the MITM key is ephemeral and not exported
3. **Active CVEs this week:** TLS terminate host header spoofing fix (PR #296, 2026-06-05), shell-quote bump to 1.8.4 (PR #301), leaf cert validity tightened (85a66578) — confirm all are in v0.x.y on npm
4. **Scope creep:** Verify `srt` cannot be used to escalate privileges or escape to the parent process namespace
5. **License compliance:** Apache-2.0 permits commercial use in our Skill Library

### Expected outcome
- `decision=green` if supply chain confirmed, MITM key ephemeral, no privilege escalation path
- `decision=red` if supply chain risk or unpatched CVE

**On green:** Set `multica issue metadata set 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 --key decision --value green`, then promote: `multica issue status 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 todo`
**On red:** Set `multica issue metadata set 14e40ee5-7b41-416e-8e46-17cc1248a9f8 --key blocked_reason --value security_review_failed`

# SECURITY REVIEW: hookify writing-rules skill import

**Dispatch sub-issue:** DEV-304 (e2a93691-025a-45cd-a3d0-4d34e6d9d6e1)
**Source URL:** https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/tree/main/plugins/hookify
**Author:** Daisy Hollman (daisy@anthropic.com)
**Type:** Claude Code skill (non-MCP) — instruction-only, no executable code imported into Multica

## What to review

### 1. License
- GitHub API returns `null` license for the `anthropics/claude-code` repo
- SKILL.md frontmatter says `license: Complete terms in LICENSE.txt`
- No LICENSE.txt found at repo root
- **Question:** Are Anthropic marketplace plugins permissible for workspace internal skill library use?
- **Expected outcome:** Anthropic's marketplace is intended for distribution — confirm internal use is permitted

### 2. Install footprint
- The hookify _plugin_ ships Python hooks (`posttooluse.py`, `pretooluse.py`, `stop.py`, `userpromptsubmit.py`)
- The _skill_ itself (`writing-rules/SKILL.md`) is instructions-only: no executables, no dependencies, no network calls
- Import scope = SKILL.md content only (not the Python hook runtime)

### 3. Security smell
- Skill teaches pattern-matching on bash commands and file edits — no exfil, no credentials access
- Rules operate read-only on hook event payloads
- Python hooks in the hookify plugin (not imported) do read/write hook stdout/stderr — not in scope for this skill import

### 4. Required scopes
- None — skill is instruction text only

## Decision required

Set `multica issue metadata set <dispatch-issue-id> --key decision --value green` on DEV-304 when review passes.
If red: set `decision=red` on DEV-304 and pin `blocked_reason=security_review_failed`.

## BENCHMARK ACTION: CuratedOneTapMCPCatalog

**Source:** Weekly Benchmark Sweep 2026-06-22 (DEV-300) — score=3.0 (frequency=1/6 vendors this week [Replit], impact=3, effort=S)

---

### Input (Pattern Observed)

Replit added Apollo (outbound prospecting/account research) and Black Forest Labs FLUX (AI image generation and editing) as one-click MCP server entries in its platform settings this week (Jun 19, 2026 changelog). Users can activate either server for their agent context with a single toggle. Combined with Bolt.new's user-configurable MCP connections (shipped ~2 weeks prior), the pattern converging across the cohort is: a curated, vendor-maintained catalog of verified MCP servers that any user can activate without writing custom integration code or editing agent config files manually.

**Current Multica gap:** MCP servers must be configured manually per agent (editing CLAUDE.md or settings files). There is no curated catalog, no one-tap activation, no security classification per server, and no verified "known-good" activation snippets shared across agents.

---

### Expected Output

A new Multica skill: **`mcp-catalog-curator`**

The skill produces and maintains a structured MCP server catalog. Each catalog entry contains:
- MCP server name and canonical URL
- Capability summary (2–3 sentences, what the server enables)
- Required environment variables (names only, not values)
- Security classification: `read-only` | `read-write` | `destructive`
- Activation snippet: a tested CLAUDE.md block that enables the server
- Verification status and date last tested

The catalog is published as a skill in the Skill Library (project 08). Any agent or workspace member can invoke the skill to browse entries, select a server, and receive a copy-paste activation snippet for their agent's CLAUDE.md.

---

### Acceptance Criteria

1. Skill `mcp-catalog-curator` is published to Skill Library (08) with a complete SKILL.md.
2. Catalog ships with a minimum of 5 verified entries (suggested initial set: Supabase, GitHub, Stripe, Apollo, Black Forest Labs FLUX).
3. Each entry has been tested and confirmed to activate without errors in at least one Multica agent environment.
4. Security classification is present for every entry.
5. Activation snippets are copy-paste ready and include no hardcoded secrets.
6. Skill is importable via `multica skill import` URL from Skill Library.

---

### Dependencies

- Supabase MCP skill research from DEV-242 / DEV-243 (prior security review) — consult for security classification guidance on Supabase entry.
- Agent must have write access to Skill Library project (08) to publish the skill.
- No external API credentials required for catalog creation (credentials are left to the activating agent).

---

### Risk

- **Low:** Catalog entries may become stale as MCP servers update; mitigation: include "last verified date" per entry and add catalog refresh to the weekly benchmark sweep scope.
- **Medium:** Security misclassification — a read-write server listed as read-only creates a false safety expectation; mitigation: err on the side of stricter classification; add a manual review step before publishing.

---

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Skill published to Skill Library (08) with SKILL.md complete
- [ ] Minimum 5 verified catalog entries
- [ ] All entries security-classified
- [ ] All activation snippets tested
- [ ] No secrets in any artifact
- [ ] `multica skill import` URL confirmed working
- [ ] Parent issue DEV-300 updated with artifact reference

issues.description:
Workspace Upgrade Weekly Loop — recursive workspace self-improvement. Mode=create_issue. Runs every Sunday 09:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3).

Run the `workspace-upgrade-report` skill against the live workspace and post a single ranked action list as a comment on the issue this autopilot creates. Auto-dispatch the top 3 actions to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, and memory-curator. Never skip a week — a silent skip is itself a finding.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the three sub-issues for top-3 dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention CEO in any comment — CEO is the assignee and re-mentioning would loop (Factory Pulse v3 no-mention rule).
- NEVER mention any agent in a wrap-up. Only mention agents to delegate first-touch work on a fresh sub-issue.
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (the workspace-operations dialog).
- Read each issue's metadata before pinning anything; do not log run state. Stay within the issue this autopilot creates.

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

(2) Collect the five input signals:

   (a) retry_logs (last 7 days). Walk every issue updated in the past 7 days and pull metadata. Any `retry_log` key present is a failure signature. Aggregate by skill name / agent / failure_reason.
       - `multica issue list --output json --status in_progress` and `--status blocked` and `--status in_review` (paged if needed).
       - For each issue, `multica issue metadata list <id> --output json` — keep only entries with a `retry_log` key.

   (b) Blocked issues — every issue currently `status=blocked`:
       - `multica issue list --status blocked --output json`
       - For each: capture id, identifier, title, assignee, age (now - updated_at), and `blocked_reason` metadata.

   (c) Stale in_review (>48h) — every issue currently `status=in_review` whose `updated_at` is more than 48h ago:
       - `multica issue list --status in_review --output json`
       - Filter client-side by `updated_at`.

   (d) Agent WIP imbalance — for every agent in the workspace, count `in_progress` issues assigned to them. Flag any agent with WIP=0 (cold) or WIP≥4 (overloaded). Flag any squad whose total WIP ≥7.
       - `multica agent list --output json`
       - `multica issue list --status in_progress --output json` and group by `assignee_id`.

   (e) Skill orphans — every skill not assigned to any agent (`agent_count==0`), and every agent with zero skills attached.
       - `multica skill list --output json`
       - For each skill, attempt `multica skill get <id> --output json` (or inspect the skill→agent join if exposed). If the CLI exposes no join command, infer from agent definitions (`multica agent get <id> --output json` per agent) — cap the scan at 30 agents per run.

(3) Score and rank actions. For each candidate upgrade action, score on:
   - Impact (how many issues/agents this would unblock or accelerate; 1–5)
   - Effort (1=trivial, 5=multi-day)
   - Confidence (1=speculative, 5=clear pattern with ≥3 occurrences)
   Final score = (Impact × Confidence) / Effort. Surface the Top 5.

(4) Compose the upgrade report comment. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue, using this exact shape so step (6) can parse it:

```
# Workspace Upgrade Report — <YYYY-MM-DD>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC)
**Signals scanned:** retry_logs=N | blocked=N | stale_in_review_48h=N | wip_outliers=N | skill_orphans=N

## Top 5 Upgrade Actions

1. **<short title>** — owner=<agent-name> | effort=<S|M|L> | impact=<1-5> | score=<n>
   - Rationale: <one sentence, cite source issue identifiers like DEV-NN>
   - Expected impact: <one sentence>

2. … (same shape)
3. … (same shape)
4. … (same shape)
5. … (same shape)

## Signal Summary
- Retry log hotspots: <skill / agent / count> …
- Stale in_review: <DEV-NN list>
- Blocked: <DEV-NN list>
- WIP outliers: <agent: count> …
- Skill orphans: <skill names>

## Next Steps
- Top 3 actions auto-dispatched to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, memory-curator (see linked sub-issues).
- Remaining actions parked on this issue for CEO triage.
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body in). Do NOT include any `mention://agent/...` link in this comment.

(5) Pin metadata on this issue ONLY if a value is materially needed by future runs. Recommended keys: `report_window_end` (RFC3339), `top_action_count` (number, expect 5), `dispatched_action_count` (number, expect 3). Use `multica issue metadata set` once each. Skip any key whose value is identical to what you already see in `metadata list`.

(6) Auto-dispatch top 3 actions. Each top-3 action gets its own sub-issue under this autopilot's created issue, assigned to the named owner. Owner mapping rules:
   - Owner agent named in the action header (skill-builder / prompt-optimizer / memory-curator) is the assignee.
   - If the top-3 already names a different agent (e.g. action #1 is a runtime fix that belongs elsewhere), still cap dispatch at exactly three sub-issues among {skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, memory-curator}. Pick the highest-scoring action that fits each agent's domain:
       * skill-builder → new skill, skill rewrite, skill orphan adoption
       * prompt-optimizer → agent prompt edit, prompt-improvement-review target, retry_log root-cause in prompts
       * memory-curator → memory cleanup, stale-fact removal, missing-default capture
   - If no candidate fits an agent's domain this week, comment on the parent: "No skill-builder/prompt-optimizer/memory-curator action this week — backlog empty for that lane." Do NOT mint a filler sub-issue.

   For each of the three dispatch sub-issues:
   - `multica issue create --title "Workspace Upgrade Action — <short title>" --project 1a1c2fb3-41ee-4b99-b820-d10f1c564052 --parent <this-issue-id> --status todo --priority high --assignee-id <agent-id> --description-stdin` with body that includes:
     * Input (the signal that triggered this)
     * Expected Output (concrete deliverable: prompt patch / new skill / memory entry)
     * Acceptance Criteria
     * Dependencies
     * Risk
     * Definition of Done
   - The sub-issue assignment fires the agent. Do not also `@mention` the agent in the sub-issue body — assignment is the trigger.

(7) Flip this issue's status to `in_review` so the CEO Command Squad can verify (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). Do NOT close it. Top-3 sub-issues stay `todo` for the owner agents.

(8) Output to stdout: `Workspace Upgrade @ <RFC3339> | top5=5 | dispatched=N | blocked=B stale=S wip_out=W orphans=O retry=R`. This is the only non-comment output.

Operating rules:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go on the auto-created issue. Never comment on DEV-36 or on issues you did not create.
- Do not use `multica issue update --status done` anywhere. The owners close their own sub-issues.
- Do not create more than 3 dispatch sub-issues. Cap is hard.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review`, never `done` — CEO verifies week-over-week trend.

issues.description:
Daily Command Center Digest. Mode=create_issue. Runs every day 07:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3). Produces ONE per-project status briefing comment on the dated issue this autopilot creates, so the CEO + workspace owner can see — at a glance — every project's state and what to push today.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues. Do not change status on any other issue.
- NEVER mention any agent or member in the digest comment. Owner reads the dated issue directly; mentions would loop (Factory Pulse no-mention rule).
- NEVER touch DEV-36 (workspace-operations dialog) — exclude it from every list, count, and section.
- Quiet on green days: if NOTHING actionable changed since yesterday's digest (no new blockers, no stale in_review, no new approval ask, no project lifecycle_phase change, no new app-request), do NOT post any comment. Leave the auto-created issue body empty so it can be auto-cancelled by step (8).
- Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

STEPS:

(1) Snapshot the workspace.
    - `multica project list --output json` → all projects.
    - `multica issue list --output json` → all issues (paged if needed).
    - For each non-cancelled issue: record id, identifier, title, status, project_id, parent_issue_id, assignee_id, assignee_type, updated_at.
    - Exclude DEV-36 (id e60d5be5-b9dd-48aa-b949-28286ea80f1b) everywhere.
    - Build agent + squad name maps once: `multica agent list --output json` and `multica squad list --output json`. Used for assignee names; never for mention links.

(2) Detect "since yesterday" deltas. Treat `since = (now - 24h)` UTC.
    - new_blockers: issues that flipped to status=blocked in the window (updated_at >= since AND status=blocked).
    - cleared_blockers: issues that left blocked in the window (status != blocked AND prior blocked_reason metadata key present — best-effort).
    - new_approval_asks: any issue whose metadata gained `waiting_on=owner` or `waiting_on=admin` in the window, or whose body/comments contain a fresh "Approval needed" line written in the window (cheap heuristic: latest comment created_at >= since and contains "approval needed" case-insensitive).
    - stale_in_review_48h: any issue status=in_review with updated_at older than 48h.
    - lifecycle_phase changes: any parent issue whose metadata `lifecycle_phase` changed (compare to value from yesterday's pin — see step 7).
    - new_app_requests: any issue created_at >= since in project `01 - App Requests` (f3089a97-c608-4599-913c-f331335e1abe).
    If ALL six lists are empty AND there is no stale in_review and no current blocked anywhere → green day. Skip to step (8) without posting.

(3) Per-project section assembly. For each project (sorted by project title prefix `00 -`, `01 -`, … and then APP projects):
    - Skip the project entirely if it has zero open issues (status in {todo, in_progress, in_review, blocked}) AND no new_app_requests AND no lifecycle change.
    - Goal: 1 sentence from project.description (truncate to ~140 chars at the first sentence boundary).
    - lifecycle_phase: read from the project's "anchor" parent issue (heuristic: the parent issue with the most children in this project; if APP project, the parent whose title starts with "APP — "). Show the value of metadata `lifecycle_phase` or "—" if unset.
    - Active issues: counts by status — `todo=N in_progress=N in_review=N blocked=N` (exclude DEV-36).
    - Blockers + waiting_on: list every issue in this project with status=blocked OR metadata `waiting_on` set. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — waiting_on=<value> | blocked_reason=<value>`. Cap 5 per project; "+N more" if truncated.
    - Next action + owner: the single highest-priority issue in this project that is `todo` or `in_progress` and not blocked. Format: `DEV-NN <title> — owner=<agent-or-squad name>` (plain name, NO mention link).
    - Risk: 1 line — pick the most material risk: stale in_review item, oldest blocker, or "WIP imbalance: <name> has N in_progress" if any agent in this project has >=4 open issues. If nothing material, write "Risk: low — no stale or overloaded items.".
    - Approval needed: yes/no. If yes, quote the exact ask (one sentence from the requesting issue's body/comment). Format: `Approval needed: yes — DEV-NN: "<exact ask>"` or `Approval needed: no`.

(4) Compose the Top-3 block. Score every open issue across the workspace by:
    - urgency: blocked=4, stale_in_review_48h=3, has approval ask=3, deploy_gate=red=4, priority=urgent=4 / high=3 / medium=2 / low=1.
    - momentum: in_progress=2, in_review=1, todo=1, backlog=0.
    - age penalty: +1 per 24h since updated_at, cap +3.
    Final score = urgency + momentum + age_penalty. Pick the top 3 distinct issues (no two from the same parent unless nothing else qualifies).

(5) Render the digest comment using this exact shape (parseable by humans and future audits):

```
# Daily Command Center Digest — <YYYY-MM-DD KW>

**Window:** <since> → <now> (UTC) | **Projects scanned:** N | **Open issues:** N | **Blocked:** N | **Stale in_review (48h):** N | **Approval asks:** N

## Per-Project Status

### <project icon> <project title>
- **Goal:** <one sentence>
- **Phase:** <lifecycle_phase>
- **Active:** todo=<n> in_progress=<n> in_review=<n> blocked=<n>
- **Blockers / waiting_on:** <list or "none">
- **Next action:** <DEV-NN title — owner=name>
- **Risk:** <one line>
- **Approval needed:** <yes/no> [— "<exact ask>"]

### <next project>
… (same shape)

## Top 3 to Push Today

1. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
2. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>
3. **DEV-NN** <title> — assignee=<name> | why: <one phrase>

## Deltas Since Yesterday
- New blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Cleared blockers: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New approval asks: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Stale in_review (48h+): <DEV-NN list or "none">
- New app requests: <DEV-NN list or "none">
- Lifecycle phase changes: <project: old→new or "none">
```

Post via: `multica issue comment add <this-issue-id> --content-stdin` (HEREDOC the body). NEVER include any `mention://agent/...` or `mention://member/...` link. Issue references should be plain `DEV-NN` text (no `mention://issue/...` either — keep the digest scannable).

(6) On a green day (step 2 short-circuited), do NOT post any comment. Skip to step (8).

(7) Pin metadata on THIS issue only if a value materially helps tomorrow's run:
    - `last_run_verdict` = "green" or "posted" (overwrite each run).
    - `last_window_end` = <RFC3339-now> (overwrite each run).
    - `lifecycle_phase_snapshot` = JSON map `{project_id: phase}` so step (2) can detect phase changes tomorrow.
    - `top3_count` = number (expect 0 on green days, 3 otherwise).
    Use `multica issue metadata set` once per changed key. Skip any key whose value is identical to what `metadata list` already shows.

(8) Status finalization.
    - If a digest was posted: flip THIS issue to `in_review` (`multica issue status <this-issue-id> in_review`). CEO + owner read it, then close.
    - If green day (no digest posted): flip THIS issue to `cancelled` with no comment so it stops cluttering the inbox.

(9) Output one line to stdout (only non-comment output): `Daily Digest @ <RFC3339> | verdict=<green|posted> | projects=N | top3=N | blocked=B stale=S approvals=A`.

OPERATING RULES:
- Never block on a single CLI failure. Log "skipped <signal>: <reason>" and continue with the remaining signals.
- All comments go ONLY on the issue this autopilot creates. Never comment on any other issue.
- Never mention any agent or member anywhere. Plain names only.
- Read this issue's existing metadata before pinning. Only update keys whose values changed.
- Do not create sub-issues, do not reassign any other issue, do not change any other issue's status.
- The created issue MUST land in `in_review` (when posted) or `cancelled` (green day) — never `done` (owner closes), never left in `todo`.

## SECURITY REVIEW: Aegis method-pack import

**Source URL:** https://github.com/GanyuanRan/Aegis
**Dispatch sub-issue:** DEV-273 (SKILL IMPORT: aegis-method-pack)

### Install footprint

- Zero npm dependencies (no package.json in core skill logic)
- Pure markdown files only
- No runtime execution — skills are guidance documents, not code
- No third-party API calls required
- No OAuth scopes required
- No network access during skill execution

### Required scopes / permissions

None. Skills are read-only markdown files that guide agent behavior. The agent reading the skill needs no elevated permissions.

### Security checklist

- [ ] No secrets or API keys embedded in skill files
- [ ] No `eval`, `exec`, or code injection vectors in skill content
- [ ] No instructions that would cause agents to exfiltrate data
- [ ] No instructions to bypass security controls (no-verify, skip-hooks, etc.)
- [ ] License confirmed MIT (copyleft-free — green)
- [ ] No malicious patterns in GitHub Actions workflows (CI only, no secrets access)
- [ ] No supply-chain risks (zero deps = zero transitive risk)
- [ ] Content review: skill instructions align with good engineering practice, not adversarial

### Files to audit

1. `skills/` directory — all 20 sub-skill markdown files
2. `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md` — host-specific guidance files
3. `.github/workflows/` — CI only, verify no secrets access

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All checklist items above verified
- [ ] No red flags found in skill content
- [ ] Set `decision=green` as metadata on this issue if approved, `decision=red` if rejected
- [ ] Comment with reasoning on this issue

### Post-review action

When done and decision=green: run `multica issue status d2ce1d15-89fa-4af7-aa1e-69c506aaa5ea todo` to promote the dispatch sub-issue (DEV-273) from backlog to todo.

### Definition of Done

Security review complete, decision metadata pinned, DEV-273 status updated accordingly.
